key: # SETTINGS.CSV  —  Controls every visual and text setting on the website.
# HOW TO EDIT:  Change only the "value" column. Never rename the "key" column.
# COMMENTS:     Any row whose key starts with # is ignored by the site.

description: Your full name — shown in the navigation bar and footer
Your professional title (used in the browser tab title)
City and country shown in the footer
Year shown in the copyright line  e.g.  © 2026

key: #   1. Go to fonts.google.com  → pick a font → click "Get font" → "Get embed code"
#   2. Copy the stylesheet URL and paste it into font_url below
#   3. Copy the CSS font-family value and paste it into font_family below
# IMPORTANT: if the URL contains commas  wrap the entire value in quotes in the file.
# To use system fonts  leave font_url empty and set font_family to:

description: Leave EMPTY — Cormorant Garamond is self-hosted from /fonts/ (GDPR). Do not paste a Google Fonts URL here.
CSS font-family value. Use quotes around names with spaces. Must match the font loaded by font_url.

key: # Use px (20px)  em (1.2em)  or clamp for responsive: clamp(16px
description: 22px)
Main reading text size (bio paragraphs  contact intro)
Size of your name in the top-left corner of the nav
Size of the Works / Bio / Contact nav links
Artwork title shown below each image in the works grid
Series label shown below the artwork title in the grid
Artwork title inside the lightbox (large overlay)
Metadata text inside the lightbox (year  material  etc.)

key: # IMPORTANT: use a dot as decimal separator here  not a comma. Write 1.88  not 1

description: Line spacing for body text. 1.0 = very tight  1.5 = normal  2.0 = airy

key: # Any valid CSS color works:  hex (#ffffff)  rgb(255 255 255)  or a name (white)

description: Main page background color
Card background color in the works grid
Card background when hovered
Primary text color (headings  titles  nav)
Secondary text color (body text  nav links)
Muted text color (labels  captions  metadata)
Subtle divider line color. rgba gives transparency control.
Left and right padding on all pages. Reduce for less white space.
Gap between gallery thumbnails. Flexible units OK e.g. clamp(28px 4vw 72px) (keep it in ONE cell). 16px tight  56px generous.
Vertical padding inside the navigation bar (top and bottom). Larger = taller nav.
Vertical padding inside the footer bar.
Minimum side padding for page content (works grid  bio  contact). Changing this does NOT affect the nav or footer — those use spacing_page_padding.

key: # The gallery is fully responsive. You control it with these knobs:
#   • columns at three screen sizes (desktop / tablet / phone)
#   • the overall max width (smaller = smaller thumbnails)
#   • the gap between thumbnails (accepts flexible units like clamp())

description: Columns on DESKTOP (wide screens).
Columns on TABLET (screens up to 1024px wide).
Columns on PHONE (screens up to 620px wide). Use 1 for one big image per row.
Max total width of the gallery. SMALLER = smaller thumbnails. Bigger = larger.
Thumbnail shape. 1/1 (square)  4/5 (portrait)  3/4  16/9 (landscape).

key: # Settings for the artwork detail view (the full-screen gallery that opens when you click a work).

description: Width of gallery images as a percentage of the viewport. 45vw = 45% of screen width.
Hard maximum width of gallery images in pixels. Prevents images from becoming too wide on very large screens.
Vertical space between images when scrolling through multiple views of a work.
Distance between the navigation dots and the left edge of the images.
Distance between the info panel and the right edge of the images.
Width of the info panel (title  series  details).
How much the image zooms when clicked. 2 = double size  3 = triple size. Applies once — click again to zoom out.
Maximum width of the bio text column. Use a smaller value for a narrower centered column.
Maximum width of the contact page content.

key: # Add images by filling in slideshow_1  slideshow_2  slideshow_3  …
# To add a caption  fill in the matching slideshow_caption_N row.
# To remove a slide  delete its row entirely or leave the value blank.
# Image paths are relative to the site folder  e.g.  images/myfile.webp

description: Darkness of the gradient shadow at the top of the slideshow (makes nav text legible). 0 = none  0.5 = half-dark  1 = solid black.
Height of the gradient shadow at the top of the slideshow.
Time between automatic slide changes in milliseconds. 5000 = 5 seconds. Set to 0 to disable auto-advance.
Duration of the crossfade transition between slides. Use ms (milliseconds).
First slideshow image — path relative to the site folder
Caption for slide 1 — leave empty for no caption
Second image path — add slideshow_3  slideshow_4  etc. as needed
Caption for slide 2
Third image path
Caption for slide 3
